metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `docs/release-notes.md` entry for 0.7.1 (noting the first end-to-end automation run of `github_release` + `mcp_registry` OIDC jobs) as part of the bump commit, and add this as a step to `docs/releasing.md`.
findings.finding: `docs/release-notes.md` is an unenumerated consumer of the version bump: it currently documents releases through 0.7.0 ("0.7.0 — GitHub Releases now auto-created on tag") and will visibly stop there after 0.7.1 ships. The plan does not mention updating it, and `docs/releasing.md` itself does not include this as a release step.
findings.location: Scope / steps section; docs/release-notes.md (top-level, currently documents through 0.7.0)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short `## 0.7.1 — First fully automated release (github_release + MCP Registry OIDC)` section to `docs/release-notes.md` in the same commit that bumps the version strings, and add "Update `docs/release-notes.md`" as a sub-bullet under step 1 of `docs/releasing.md`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Pin the mcp-publisher download to a specific release version (e.g. `releases/download/v1.2.3/`) rather than `releases/latest`, and record the pinned version in the workflow comment, so the CLI interface is stable across the Gerrit-merge-to-tag-push window.
findings.finding: The mcp-publisher binary is downloaded using `releases/latest` (unpinned), so the CLI interface, binary naming convention, or the tar.gz archive structure could change between pre-release testing and the actual v0.7.1 tag push without any repo-side signal.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `releases/latest/download/` with `releases/download/<pinned-version>/` in the curl URL and add a comment noting the version; update when intentionally upgrading.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the smoke test for AC #10: specify the exact command(s) to run (e.g., `pip install nava-rebar==0.7.1 && rebar --version`), the target environment, and the expected output that constitutes 'passes'.
findings.finding: The 'post-release smoke test' in both the Scope bullets and AC #10 ('The post-release smoke test passes against the published 0.7.1 artifact') is undefined: no command, no target, no pass/fail criterion. An executing agent cannot run or evaluate it without guessing.
findings.location: Scope / steps bullet 5; Acceptance Criteria AC #10
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC #10 with: 'Running `pip install nava-rebar==0.7.1 && rebar --version` in a clean virtualenv returns `rebar 0.7.1` (or equivalent CLI banner) with exit code 0.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm (or update) `docs/releasing.md` to remove/replace any manual GitHub Release creation and interactive MCP Registry login steps, reflecting that both are now fully automated by the `github_release` and `mcp_registry` workflow jobs.
findings.finding: The plan follows docs/releasing.md but does not include a step to verify (or update) that file to reflect the newly automated GitHub Release and MCP Registry flows. Since v0.7.0 required a manual GitHub Release ('manual one-off') and v0.7.1 is the first automated one, releasing.md may still describe manual steps for GitHub Release creation, leaving it stale after v0.7.1 ships.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a post-release step: 'Verify docs/releasing.md no longer references manual GitHub Release creation or interactive MCP Registry login; update if needed to match the automated v0.7.1 flow.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a recovery runbook (even a short bullet list) for each automated job failure: which jobs can be safely re-triggered, which require manual fallback, and what state to verify before retrying.
findings.finding: No operator recovery procedure is specified for partial-pipeline failure: if github_release or mcp_registry job fails after PyPI has already published, the plan gives no actionable steps (retry workflow? manual GitHub Release? manual registry push?), leaving the operator without a runbook for the first production run of new automation.
findings.location: Scope / steps — tag push pipeline; Verification note
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Failure recovery' subsection under Scope/steps: (1) If github_release fails: re-run that single job (it calls gh release create which is idempotent if the release doesn't yet exist, errors gracefully if it does). (2) If mcp_registry fails: re-run the job; OIDC tokens are per-job so a fresh token is issued on retry. (3) If PyPI publish fails and is retried after a partial upload, use `twine upload --skip-existing` or note that a re-upload of the same version will be rejected.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document (or configure) idempotent PyPI upload behavior — either confirm the workflow uses `--skip-existing` / `twine upload --skip-existing`, or note that a partial failure after PyPI acceptance requires manually triggering only the downstream jobs.
findings.finding: PyPI publish is non-idempotent: re-uploading the same version (0.7.1) after a partial workflow failure returns HTTP 400 'File already exists'. The plan does not acknowledge this constraint or specify how to recover (e.g., --skip-existing flag, or prohibition on re-running the full workflow).
findings.location: Scope / steps — 'publish (PyPI, OIDC)'; Acceptance Criteria — 'PyPI shows nava-rebar 0.7.1 live'
findings.suggested_fix: In the release.yml publish step, add `--skip-existing` to the twine/upload action so a retry after a partial failure proceeds past PyPI to the github_release and mcp_registry jobs without a 400 error.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that Gerrit changes 316 (github_release GH_REPO fix) and 319 (mcp_registry OIDC job) are both merged to main before tagging v0.7.1, and record the merge SHAs as evidence on this ticket.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'the fixed github_release job (GH_REPO; change 316)' and 'the new mcp_registry job (GitHub Actions OIDC; change 319)' are already landed, but no grep evidence of Gerrit change IDs '316' or '319' exists anywhere in the repository. These changes are the foundational premise of the entire plan — if either hasn't actually merged to main, the automation this release is designed to exercise doesn't exist yet.
findings.location: Plan section 'What' — 'the fixed github_release job (GH_REPO; change 316)' and 'the new mcp_registry job (GitHub Actions OIDC; change 319)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a pre-tagging gate step: run `git log --oneline main | head -20` and cross-reference the commit messages to confirm both changes landed; alternatively link the merged Gerrit change URLs directly in this ticket's description.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Pin `mcp-publisher` to a specific released version (e.g. `v0.x.y`) in the `curl` install URL and add a sha256 checksum verification step, or switch to the official `modelcontextprotocol/registry` GitHub Action if one exists, to match the established pinned-dependency pattern for third-party CI tools.
findings.finding: The mcp_registry job installs `mcp-publisher` from `releases/latest` (floating version, no pinned tag, no checksum/cosign verification). This deviates from the established pattern of pinning third-party CLI tools to a specific version + integrity hash in CI, with no justification given. If the `latest` binary changes behavior or is compromised between this plan's writing and the tag push, the job may silently publish incorrect metadata or fail in an undiagnosed way.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the floating `releases/latest` URL with a pinned version: e.g. `MCP_PUB_VERSION=v0.1.0` (check current release), then verify `echo '<expected-sha256>  mcp-publisher' | sha256sum -c` after extraction. Alternatively, check if `https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/registry` now publishes an official composite Action that can be referenced at a pinned SHA.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm `mcp-publisher login github-oidc` and `mcp-publisher publish` subcommands exist in the current published binary by running `curl -L <download-url> | tar xz mcp-publisher && ./mcp-publisher --help` on a local Linux box (or in a workflow_dispatch dry-run step) before pushing the v0.7.1 tag.
findings.finding: The `mcp-publisher login github-oidc` subcommand — the central new capability this release exercises — is UNVERIFIED: the binary is downloaded at runtime from github.com/modelcontextprotocol/registry/releases/latest, and no local copy or --help output exists in the repo to confirm the subcommand name matches what the published binary actually exposes.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a preflight step to the mcp_registry job (or a standalone workflow_dispatch dry-run) that runs `./mcp-publisher --help` and `./mcp-publisher login --help` and prints the output, so the subcommand names are confirmed in CI logs before the real tag push. Alternatively, pin the mcp-publisher download to a specific release tag (not 'latest') so the CLI interface is stable.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
